00:27:18:14 \- 00:27:39:05

Code. So dependency injection, it is like implementation of dependency inversion principle. And it is like the last of those principles. And basically it says that high level modules like in the best scenario, if you have two modules and one in between like okay, yeah, let's start from two, you have two parts of your UI. Let's say, or your application, you have some module or component and you have some kind of library.

00:12:23:28 \- 00:12:54:21  
Speaker 3  
So that high level module should not depend on low level, because when you have tight coupling between, like business logic and for example, some libraries, one library like for example, mom and JS, I don't know if you've heard about it gets deprecated. Then you have to rewrite all of your logic. So first you separate it and then you have modules in between, like modules of abstraction.

00:12:54:23 \- 00:13:24:18  
Speaker 3  
And it is called inversion of control because now instead of depending down, both of them depend on what's like in between. And this abstraction like makes it secure because you don't have to rewrite your business logic, you just have to adapt. For example, by using fact, our our proxy.
